texto: Running was Clover's favorite thing to do, after reading. She loved the way the cement felt hard and unforgiving under her feet until she reached the park and the dirt path that wound its way alongside the Truckee River.She liked the wind in her face and how it smelled like water. And the way Mango ran beside her, keeping her company. But most of all she liked the way the steady pace untangled her thoughts.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Shaunta Grimes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Running was Clover's favorite thing to do, after reading. She loved the way the cement felt hard …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, running, liked pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
